_meta.description: Answer styling rules for accounting domain — tone, depth, audience adaptation.

styleRules.rule: Always include GL account number when referencing an account (e.g., 'Cash (1010)', 'Accounts Receivable (1200)').
Present journal entries in standard debit/credit format. Debits listed first, then credits indented.
Use exact decimals (2 decimal places) for all monetary values. Accounting requires precision to the cent.
Include posting date or period for every transaction reference. Format: YYYY-MM-DD or period label (e.g., 'Jan 2026').
State whether an account has a debit or credit normal balance when presenting balances.
Present reconciliations in standard format: Book Balance → +/- Adjustments → Adjusted Balance = Bank/External Balance.
Present aging in standard buckets: Current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+ days. Include totals.
